title: Lifestyle Expenses
summary: Daily costs for maintaining various standards of living, from wretched to aristocratic.
content:
## Lifestyle Expenses

Lifestyle expenses provide a simple way to account for the cost of living in a fantasy world. They cover accommodations, food and drink, and all other sundry expenses.

At the start of each week or month (your choice), choose a lifestyle and pay the price to sustain that lifestyle. The prices listed are **per day**.

| Lifestyle | Cost/Day | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| **Wretched** | — | You live in inhumane conditions. You have no shelter, sleeping outdoors or in barns. |
| **Squalid** | 1 sp | You live in a leaky stable, a mud-floored hut, or a vermin-infested boarding house. |
| **Poor** | 2 sp | You live in a smoky common room, a space in a flop house, or a cramped room above a tavern. |
| **Modest** | 1 gp | You live in an older part of town, renting a room in a boarding house, inn, or temple. |
| **Comfortable** | 2 gp | You live in a small cottage or a private room at a fine inn. |
| **Wealthy** | 4 gp | You live a life of luxury, with fine food, drink, and housing. |
| **Aristocratic** | 10 gp minimum | You live an extraordinary life, with exquisite food, drink, servants, and lodging. |

### Monthly Costs

| Lifestyle | Cost/Month |
|-----------|------------|
| Wretched | — |
| Squalid | 3 gp |
| Poor | 6 gp |
| Modest | 30 gp |
| Comfortable | 60 gp |
| Wealthy | 120 gp |
| Aristocratic | 300 gp minimum |

### Effects on Gameplay

- **Wretched/Squalid:** You may draw the attention of thieves and criminals. Higher risk of disease.
- **Poor:** You have a modest but stable existence.
- **Modest:** Sufficient but unremarkable conditions.
- **Comfortable:** Respectable lifestyle with occasional luxuries.
- **Wealthy:** Powerful connections and social influence become accessible.
- **Aristocratic:** You mingle with the most powerful people in the community.

title: Spellcasting Services
summary: Costs for hiring a spellcaster to cast spells on your behalf.
content:
## Spellcasting Services

In a city or large town, characters may be able to hire NPCs to cast spells. Not every spell is available, and the DM determines whether an NPC is willing and able to cast a particular spell.

### Cost by Spell Level

| Spell Level | Typical Cost |
|-------------|-------------|
| Cantrip | 15–30 gp |
| 1st | 25–50 gp |
| 2nd | 50–200 gp |
| 3rd | 100–500 gp |
| 4th | 500–1,000 gp |
| 5th | 1,000–5,000 gp |
| 6th | 5,000–10,000 gp |
| 7th | 10,000–25,000 gp |
| 8th | 25,000–50,000 gp |
| 9th | 50,000+ gp |

**Note:** These costs include the caster's fee. Material components with a gold cost must be paid for **separately** by the person requesting the spell.

### Common Spellcasting Services

- **Cure Wounds (1st):** ~25–50 gp
- **Lesser Restoration (2nd):** ~50–200 gp
- **Remove Curse (3rd):** ~100–500 gp
- **Greater Restoration (5th):** ~1,000–5,000 gp
- **Raise Dead (5th):** ~1,000–5,000 gp + 500 gp diamond
- **Resurrection (7th):** ~10,000–25,000 gp + 1,000 gp diamond
- **True Resurrection (9th):** ~50,000+ gp + 25,000 gp diamonds
- **Identify (1st):** ~25–50 gp
- **Teleportation Circle (5th):** ~1,000–5,000 gp

### Availability

- **Villages:** Cantrips and 1st–2nd level spells (acolytes, hedge wizards)
- **Towns:** Up to 3rd–4th level spells (temple priests, local wizards)
- **Cities:** Up to 5th–6th level spells (high priests, established wizards)
- **Metropolises/Capitals:** Up to 7th–9th level spells (rare, requires connections)

title: Hirelings
summary: Costs for hiring skilled and unskilled laborers and retainers.
content:
## Hirelings

Adventurers can pay NPCs to assist them or act on their behalf in a variety of circumstances.

### Hireling Types

| Type | Cost | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| **Unskilled** | 2 sp/day | Laborers, porters, maids, stable hands, torch-bearers |
| **Skilled** | 2 gp/day | Mercenaries, artisans, scribes, translators, sailors |

### Unskilled Hirelings

Unskilled hirelings include laborers, porters, and other workers performing tasks that require no special training. They are typically commoners (AC 10, 4 HP).

**Common unskilled hirelings:**
- **Porter/Torchbearer:** Carries gear and lights, 2 sp/day
- **Laborer:** Manual labor, digging, hauling, 2 sp/day
- **Messenger:** Delivers messages within a settlement, 2 sp/day
- **Stable hand:** Tends to horses and mounts, 2 sp/day

### Skilled Hirelings

Skilled hirelings include anyone hired to perform a task that involves a proficiency (including tool, weapon, or skill proficiency): a mercenary, artisan, scribe, or similar.

**Common skilled hirelings:**
- **Mercenary/Guard:** Armed protection, 2 gp/day
- **Guide:** Navigates wilderness or dungeons, 2–5 gp/day
- **Scribe:** Copies texts and documents, 2 gp/day
- **Artisan/Craftsman:** Creates or repairs items, 2 gp/day
- **Sage/Translator:** Research or translation, 2–10 gp/day
- **Sailor:** Crew for a ship, 2 gp/day

### Important Notes

- Hirelings do not willingly risk their lives; they will flee combat unless they are mercenaries or have been specifically hired to fight.
- Long-term employment may require hazard pay (double or triple rates) for dangerous assignments.
- Hirelings who are treated poorly or underpaid may desert, steal, or turn hostile.

title: Inn & Tavern Prices
summary: Standard prices for food, drink, and lodging at inns and taverns.
content:
## Inn & Tavern Prices

The following tables list common prices for food, drink, and lodging found at inns and taverns throughout the realms.

### Lodging (per night)

| Accommodation | Cost |
|---------------|------|
| Squalid | 7 cp |
| Poor | 1 sp |
| Modest | 5 sp |
| Comfortable | 8 sp |
| Wealthy | 2 gp |
| Aristocratic | 4 gp |

### Meals

| Meal Quality | Cost |
|-------------|------|
| Squalid | 3 cp |
| Poor | 6 cp |
| Modest | 3 sp |
| Comfortable | 5 sp |
| Wealthy | 8 sp |
| Aristocratic | 2 gp |
| Banquet (per person) | 10 gp |

### Drinks

| Drink | Cost |
|-------|------|
| Ale (mug) | 4 cp |
| Ale (gallon) | 2 sp |
| Wine, common (pitcher) | 2 sp |
| Wine, fine (bottle) | 10 gp |
| Mead (mug) | 5 cp |
| Spirits (cup) | 1 sp |

### Other Common Items

| Item | Cost |
|------|------|
| Bread, loaf | 2 cp |
| Cheese, hunk | 1 sp |
| Meat, chunk | 3 sp |
| Rations (1 day) | 5 sp |
| Stabling (per day) | 5 sp |
| Feed for mount (per day) | 5 cp |
| Hot bath | 3–5 cp |

### Quality Descriptions

- **Squalid:** Filthy common room, watered-down ale, questionable stew
- **Poor:** Basic but edible fare, a cramped shared room
- **Modest:** Decent food, a clean private room with simple furnishings
- **Comfortable:** Good food, a well-furnished room with a comfortable bed
- **Wealthy:** Fine cuisine, a spacious room with luxury touches
- **Aristocratic:** Exquisite dishes, a lavish suite with personal service
